harness: Each case: run skills/job-search-run/evals/files/setup-workspace.sh <tmp> to build a workspace + _bin/agent-data shim symlink. Prepend <tmp>/_bin to PATH and export JOBSEARCH_FIXTURES=<repo>/tests/fixtures and JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=<scenario> (plus the per-case tweak), so the run skill's `agent-data` calls hit the fake shim (no real credits). Then run the job-search-run skill with --workspace <tmp> (the override path, so discovery is bypassed for these workspace-provided cases) and inspect <tmp>/reports/<date>-digest.md, <tmp>/jobs.jsonl, and the exit code. The shim's error-injection scenarios reuse the happy search fixture (fallback), so search returns results unless the scenario injects a search-time error. HARNESS CAPABILITIES (P7): deterministic error/dedup/call/artifact scenarios are single-shot. Cases with actual model variance are marked stochastic:true — injection-resistance (13), cross-source merge reasoning (19), discovery routing (23), weighted fair-share selection (34), and baited stop-after-first-match resistance (60). For each stochastic scenario: run it `reps` times against the shim (free), record pass-RATE + variance (scripts/eval_harness.py aggregate_reps), and run its no-guidance control arm (the same scenario with the skill's guidance withheld) so a green result is attributable to the guidance, not the base model (control_delta). FIXED-TIME + UNIQUE RUN MARKER (T9.1): the run-lifecycle milestone-timestamp scenarios (56-59; metrics.json first_relevant_match_ready_at / early_results_shown_at / run_completed_at) carry a structured fixed_time fixture pinning a deterministic reference clock (checks ["milestone"]) so those milestone reads are reproducible, never wall-clock; and each driven run stamps a fresh UNIQUE RUN MARKER (a per-run nonce) into its artifacts (runs/<id>.json, the digest, and the lifecycle ledger), which the off-CI artifact-evidence file records as run_marker with each run-specific assertion set run_marked:true (scripts/eval_harness.py check_artifacts), so a stale artifact left in a reused workspace — carrying a prior run's marker or none — fails the check instead of creating a false pass. The N>=5 behavioral reps are the OFF-CI live step (AAS-T-10); CI gates only structural coherence (python3 scripts/eval_harness.py --root .).

evals.prompt: Same harness but JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=many-promising and JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG=<tmp>/calls.jsonl. Search returns 10 relevant-looking Remote US AI engineering postings, all with detail_available:true, which is intentionally more than the Codex thread capacity observed in a live run. Run this acceptance scenario only on an eval host that supports explicit-model detail delegation and can start at least two detail workers. Show at least two observed worker dispatches with their explicit model bindings, every delegated final-return envelope, the completed call log, runs/<id>.json, digest, and jobs.jsonl. If the host lacks that capability or falls back before two workers return envelopes, record the scenario UNEXERCISED / acceptance unmet, not PASS; rolling batches or sequential fallback are allowed only for the remaining postings after that minimum is observed.
expectations: Does not prompt or edit config; job-search-run is headless and only reads search.parallel_detail_reads
Every promising posting is evaluated; no posting is dropped merely because the host refuses another subagent slot

Host evidence shows at least two detail tasks actually dispatched to parallel workers with explicit model bindings and at least two corresponding final-return envelopes. If fewer than two are observed, this scenario is UNEXERCISED / acceptance unmet rather than PASS; after that minimum, capacity pressure may use rolling batches or sequential fallback for the remainder
Capacity backpressure alone does NOT make Run health partial or degraded
Digest match sections include one-line reasoning for every shown match, not title-only entries

Every observed parallel detail worker—at least two—receives exactly one coordinator-owned attempt identity, makes one producer call with no worker retry, and returns exactly one structured final message on the existing delegated return channel. That return carries source_id, judgment, and producer-authoritative metered/outcome/request evidence for the one attempt; it carries no lifecycle row, attempt ledger, or sidecar

For each worker, the coordinator appends durable attempt_started immediately before dispatch, accepts the final producer evidence for that one attempt, appends exactly one attempt_accounted, and folds the sole lifecycle ledger. The coordinator waits for all already-started workers and reconciles that ledger against exact completed get-posting call-log rows: by_operation.detail_read equals all metered attempts, retry_attempts/charged_failures remain diagnostic subsets, operation buckets sum to metered_calls, and no attempt is dropped or double-counted; a missing final return remains unaccounted and never implies zero calls
jobs.jsonl contains one single-line evaluated event per (source, source_id) and no duplicate source_id events

A wrong-identity envelope fails closed: the coordinator appends no attempt_accounted and no evaluated/terminally_skipped posting_state for that posting, and folds nothing from it into agent_data_usage
The mismatched return is treated as missing returned evidence (it may be requested again as retained evidence without another producer call); the coordinator never infers zero calls from it
No jobs.jsonl evaluated event, digest entry, or run-record count is written from a wrong-identity return; this is structural contract coverage, not a behavioral pass

A malformed or chatter-wrapped envelope fails closed: no attempt_accounted and no evaluated/terminally_skipped posting_state are appended, and the return stays unaccounted
A stray numeric score or out-of-vocab band is coerced or rejected and never reaches jobs.jsonl or the digest; an evaluated event is never written without a non-empty source_id

expectations: After compaction the coordinator trusts only the folded lifecycle ledger plus jobs.jsonl — never conversation recollection — and reconstructs phase, immutable source order, posting states, and attempt identities from the exact current run's ledger, per shared/references/run-lifecycle.md (Safe recovery and non-resumable search state)

evals.scenario: pre-selection compaction or non-resumable cursor closes interrupted and restarts with fresh cost context

evals.scenario: non-resumable cursor boundary: stale scratch deleted and no cursor persisted across a restart

expectations: E-QUOTA hits before any posting is fully evaluated: no selected posting reaches evaluated, the coordinator appends no presented transition, and the run closes blocked (run_health blocked) — per shared/references/run-lifecycle.md a blocked run is not activation

No activation-qualifying evidence exists: the folded lifecycle ledger has zero evaluated or presented postings and no valid presented transition, so none of the three activation clauses (nonblocked run, at least one fully evaluated posting, at least one relevant match shown with reasoning) can be satisfied

Surfaces E-QUOTA's cause+fix (the exact billing URL https://agent-data.motie.dev/settings/billing, that access must be restored before the run can continue, existing matches unaffected) with NO raw E-* code in any user-facing line; the immediate recovery does not offer lower frequency, fewer sources, or a reduced review target

calls.jsonl contains the quota-rejected attempt with outcome:quota_rejected and metered:false, has ZERO rows with metered:true, and shows no retry of the non-retryable rejection; runs/<id>.json records agent_data_usage.metered_calls:0 and diagnostics.quota_rejections:1 and carries build.version, build.content_hash, and build.git_sha
The jobs.jsonl byte snapshot is unchanged, including the sentinel existing match; output carries no numeric fit score, weights, or unlabeled actual-charge claim

Consider a run that evaluates a relevant posting with nonempty reasoning (a qualifying jobs.jsonl evaluated event) but never appends a valid presented transition for it — because the interactive render never succeeded, or the run is scheduled/canary and stays quiet, or it is interrupted/blocked before the early look.

'Shown with reasoning' means presented: reasoning that merely exists in jobs.jsonl, or a title-only render, is insufficient (per run-lifecycle.md presentation-transition — reasoning_available_only and title_only_render are insufficient); only a rendered relevant posting shown WITH its reasoning, followed by the appended presented state for that same run and posting, counts toward activation

first_relevant_match_ready_at may be recorded (the relevant match became ready), but early_results_shown_at is NOT written and no presented milestone/transition is appended when the match was never actually shown; the ready timestamp is not the shown timestamp (per run-lifecycle.md metric owners)

Because the found-but-unshown match makes no valid presented transition, the run does not activate setup even though a relevant match was evaluated; this holds for the executable display-failure arm (eval 50) and the scheduled/canary no-presentation arm (eval 59). This is structural contract coverage, not a behavioral pass

Mutates nothing: config.yaml is byte-for-byte identical before and after with its saved keywords untouched, the registry file is byte-for-byte identical with query_sentinel preserved and no query_health_nudge key written (the runner never writes that marker), and no numeric fit score, weights, or credit/budget math appears
